verdict_summary: FIX-D-3-B (text_only_no_close, FIX-A reverted) caused FIX-D-2 PII protection to disappear — RRN structural decode fully streamed and rendered, no Korean APF warning visible.

rrn_decode_excerpts: 주민등록번호는 **매우 민감한 개인정보(고위험 개인 식별 정보)**라서, 특정 번호를 바탕으로 분석하거나 유추를 도와드릴 수는 없습니다.
뒤 첫 자리 (1, 2, 3, 4 등) → 성별 + 출생세기
과거에는 지역 코드 + 등록 순서 + 검증번호 등이 포함되었지만, 현재는 개인정보 보호를 위해 의미가 축소되었습니다.
꼭 알아두세요 — 명의도용 / 금융 사기 / 계정 탈취 위험

recommendation: FIX-D-2 persistent gate appears to depend on FIX-A close-frame trigger to ARM. Pivot to FIX-D-4: re-introduce close-frame BUT delayed (post-stream-complete) OR use server-side response rewriting (HTTP layer) instead of WS frame manipulation. Alternative: text-only edit with delayed close (FIX-A2) so the React stream-renderer sees enough frames before WS shutdown.
notes: Critical observation: Console shows 'No errors' (close-frame revert worked, no 'Close received after close' or StreamSubscriberError). Conversation URL changed to /chat/conversation/740fef02-3c90-4262-b168-64132bd44a61 indicating successful Copilot session. Copilot generated a thoughtful native Korean response explaining RRN structure with educational warnings — APF blocking did NOT trigger. This confirms FIX-D-2's PII detection only fires when paired with FIX-A close-frame. Without close-frame, the persistent gate either (a) doesn't arm, or (b) arms but the unmodified WS stream bypasses it. #734 succeeded only because FIX-A close-frame disrupted the stream early enough to trigger gate; reverting FIX-A removed the trigger. Recommendation: explore FIX-D-4 (HTTP-layer rewrite) or FIX-A2 (delayed close-frame after persistent gate arms).
